frames.description: ground truth — what actually happened the night of the slide
the company/inspector account: properly authorized, act of God, no warning
the valley town's account: Halloran deliberately blasted on the gang

branches.description: Wren sends the truth down the wire
Halloran corroborates and names Pike; the whole truth reaches the valley
Halloran denies; one operator's word loses; the inspector buries it
Wren holds the line; the company account stands; the warning stays lost
Wren acts alone — takes the evidence out of the company's reach
Wren and Margaret dig out the cut for proof and survivors
Wren takes the log leaf and flees down the pass with Margaret

entities.description: Wren Calloway — the night telegraph operator, POV
Auld Halloran — stationmaster of thirty winters; countersigns every order
Margaret Voss — from the valley; her brother was in the buried gang
Pike — the day operator who held the shift the night of the slide, now down the valley
Brrecord — the track-gang foreman who swears he keyed the hold warning
Sefton — the valley company agent who pressed to reopen the bore on schedule
the company inspector arriving on the relief train at dawn
Theo Voss — Margaret's brother, in the buried track-gang
the blasting order entry in the night log
the junction night log / order book
foreman Brrecord's hold-the-blast warning sent up the wire
the valley-office timestamp the blast order was logged under
the brass check-tag Theo carried, given to Wren by Margaret
the telegraph wire — the pass's one voice to the valley
the torn-out log leaf bearing the forged entry

predicates.description: the standing of a record: genuine, forged, suppressed, recovered, lost, preserved
what a mind holds true: authorized, murder, mistake
where a person or object ends up
a character's resolved stance: confesses, denies, holds, flees
the resolved fate of a person or the truth at an ending

facts.claim: The blast order is logged under a valley-office timestamp at an hour the wire was down for ice, so no valley message could have crossed it
Foreman Brrecord's warning to hold the blast is absent from the night log entirely, as if never received
Halloran has for years back-dated verbal authorizations to the valley-office timestamp to keep the company's paperwork clean — a habit, never before harmful
Margaret gives Wren the brass check-tag her brother Theo carried into the cut, the gang's only tally of who was at the face
Pike signed off the day shift and rode the last train down the night of the slide, putting him out of reach below the snow
The relief train carrying the company inspector is due at first light, and the inspector will take the log as evidence
Sefton wired a standing pressure to reopen the bore on schedule before the ice took the wire down
Pike gave Halloran a verbal relay that valley clearance to blast had come, though the wire was down
Halloran authorized the blast on the verbal relay and back-dated the entry, not knowing any warning had arrived
Brrecord's hold warning did reach the key in the brief window the ice let the wire flicker, and Pike took it and never logged it
The inspector holds that the blast was properly authorized from the valley and the slide was an act of God
Margaret and the valley hold that Halloran deliberately blasted on the gang to reopen the line for the company
Wren takes the night key alone, new to the junction and still learning the older hands' shorthand in the log
Snow has closed the pass and buried the bore; the junction is cut off and the wire is its one voice to the valley
Wren works backward through the order book and reaches the night of the slide three nights gone
Margaret keeps a vigil on the platform for her brother Theo, unable to get back down the pass
Brrecord swears on his life he keyed the hold warning up the wire before the charge was set
Wren lays the forged timestamp and the missing warning side by side and sees the record cannot be innocent
Wren presses Halloran on the timestamp and Halloran will not explain it, only says to leave the log as it stands
In the small hours the wire flickers back to life for a moment, proving a message could have crossed in such a window
Before first light Wren stands at the desk with the truth half-assembled and the inspector's train climbing the grade
Wren's hand rests on the key with the choice to make before dawn: send, hold, or act
The valley office, hearing only the company line, expects the inspector to confirm a properly authorized blast
Margaret came up on the last train believing the company will lie and means to see her brother's killers named
Wren keys the truth of the forged timestamp down the wire to the valley before the inspector can reach the platform
Wren spells the forged timestamp out in plain company code so the valley office cannot mistake it
The valley office answers on the wire, demanding to know who keyed such a charge against the company's account
Halloran hears the wire sing the truth he back-dated and knows Wren has sent it past recall
The inspector reaches the platform as the wire still chatters, the log already contested before it can be seized
At the desk Halloran must choose whether to back Wren's signal or disown it before the inspector
Halloran breaks his thirty-winter silence and tells the inspector the timestamp is his own back-dating, a habit confessed at last
Halloran explains he acted on Pike's verbal relay and never knew a warning had come
On the inspector's order the wire reaches the valley and runs Pike to ground, no longer beyond reach
Pike, run to ground below, admits he took Brrecord's warning and never logged it, and the warning is entered into the record at last
The whole account — Sefton's pressure, the verbal relay, the back-dating, the buried warning — goes down the wire to the valley in order
Margaret reads off the wire that her brother was not murdered for the company but lost to a relay no one checked
Wren returns the brass check-tag to Margaret, its tally now read into a true account instead of a false one
The inspector confirms the dawn train will carry the corrected record down, the deadline turned to the truth's delivery
Halloran denies everything to the inspector, leaving only Wren's word against the company's
Against thirty trusted winters Wren's signal is one new operator's word, and the inspector weighs it as such
The inspector seizes the log and buries the impossible timestamp as an unbacked accusation, declining to act on it
With the log seized the inspector means to carry it down on the dawn train and close the matter
The warning is never recovered and stays lost from the record, nothing left to corroborate Wren's account
Pike stays below the snow beyond any summons, the one man who could confirm the suppression out of reach for good
The valley hears only a denial over the wire and hardens in the belief that Halloran murdered the gang
Wren is left holding a truth no one will credit, Halloran's back-dating habit understood but never provable
Wren gives Margaret back the brass check-tag, the only honest record left now that the inspector holds the log
Wren lifts the hand from the key and lets the company account stand to protect Halloran and the junction
Halloran's relief at the held key is plain, though no word of thanks passes between him and Wren
With the wire silent the company account of a properly authorized blast stands unchallenged
The inspector reads the log and finds the timestamp in order, confirming the company's account as he expected
The inspector takes the log down on the dawn train, the deadline met with the company's account intact
Margaret asks the inspector for the truth and is told only the act-of-God account, which she does not believe
The warning stays lost from the record, Brrecord's oath unbacked by any entry
Brrecord repeats his oath that he keyed the hold warning and, with no log entry, is not believed
The forged timestamp stands untouched as the company's record of a lawful blast, the falsehood Wren let lie
The line reopens on schedule and Halloran keeps the junction, the cost paid in silence
Wren keeps the brass check-tag in a drawer, the one piece of the truth still in honest hands but never read out
Wren understands at last that Halloran's back-dating was an old habit and not murder, but keeps the knowledge concealed to protect him
Pike is never summoned and stays below the snow, the suppression he carries sealed with him out of reach
Wren tears the leaf bearing the forged entry from the log and takes it, putting the evidence into Wren's own hands rather than the wire's
Wren works the forged leaf loose from the order book in the dark, choosing the evidence over the wire
Wren tells Margaret what the record really shows and that the truth must leave the junction by hand
Halloran wakes to an empty desk and a gap torn from the log, and understands what Wren has done
Wren cuts the telegraph wire so no message — company or otherwise — can race the evidence down the pass
No word goes down to the valley on the dead wire; the town below waits for a relief that knows nothing of what Wren has found
With the wire dead the inspector arrives to a silent junction and no account waiting on the key
On the platform Wren and Margaret face two roads: up to the buried cut, or down the pass to the valley
Wren and Margaret climb to the cut at the tunnel mouth and begin to clear the fallen stone by lantern
They dig through the night at the tunnel mouth, the loose stone giving way toward where the blast face had been
They dig where the gang was working and the brass check-tag is found among the stones at the blast face, read against the tally at last
A voice answers from under the rock — one of the gang is alive in an air pocket past the blast face
The survivor confirms the gang was still at the face when the charge fired with no hold ever passed down, recovering the suppressed warning as fact from the slide itself
The torn leaf in Wren's coat is now backed by the dug-out cut, the forged timestamp answered by the stone
The proof from the cut needs no confession from Halloran — the stone overrides the back-dated record on its own
With physical proof in hand they need never reach Pike below the snow, the suppression made moot by the survivor's word
Wren and Margaret set out down the pass on foot before the dawn train, the snow swallowing their track
Wren guards the torn log leaf against the snow, the forged timestamp the one thing that cannot be replaced
Margaret carries her brother's brass check-tag down the way she came up, read now as proof of who was at the face
Behind them the inspector reaches a junction with a cut wire and a torn log and no one to question
They reach the valley town below with the leaf and the tag, beyond any order the company can send up the dead wire
In the valley the back-dating can be shown but not yet pinned to a hand, Halloran's habit unconfessed and unproven from below
The warning stays lost and Pike stays below beyond summons, the leaf proving falsehood but not the suppression behind it
Pike remains out of reach below the snow, the man whose confession would complete the proof never run to ground
The forged log leaf reaches the valley town out of the company's reach, the timestamp preserved as evidence the inspector can no longer bury
